compiled_state.author: RebarBotNava
compiled_state.description:
# RC2b: snapshot-horizon-safe replay (no silent sub-horizon drop)

## Why
When two clones write at once, one can compact and delete the events it folded before seeing the other's event.
After merge, that event sorts BEFORE the snapshot and replay's positional skip drops it regardless of
`source_event_uuids` -> silent data loss. Live store holds ~11 such orphans.

## Decision (ACCEPTED): Option 1 (rebuild-on-stray) + Option 3 (conservative horizon)
Option 2 (per-field LWW-by-timestamp) deferred.

## Concrete rules
- SETTLED (Option 3): compaction folds an event only if `hlc.physical_now() - event_ts >= COMPACTION_HORIZON_NS`.
  `COMPACTION_HORIZON_NS` is a NEW `CompactConfig` field (default `1_800_000_000_000` = 1800 s in HLC nanosecond
  ticks — HLC uses `time.time_ns()`); ALSO add its coercer entry to `_SECTIONS['compact']` in `_config_schema.py`
  and an env-var alias in `config.py` (the established pattern). Younger "hot-edge" events stay live `.json`.
- REBUILD (Option 1): fsck detects a merged-in pre-snapshot orphan (KNOWN-type `.json` sorting before the SNAPSHOT,
  uuid absent from `source_event_uuids`) and rebuilds that ticket's snapshot from the full ordered log INCLUDING
  `.retired`. This needs a NEW `include_retired: bool = False` param on `reduce_ticket` (`reducer/_api.py`) +
  `prepare_event_files` (`reducer/_cache.py`, both listing paths ~97/~102) + `scan_for_latest_snapshot` seeing the
  same set. CACHE (resolved): the rebuild path BYPASSES the `compute_dir_hash` cache and reads the full file set
  directly (avoids a stale `*.json`-only hash ignoring `.retired`). Rebuild uses `fsutil.atomic_write` (the existing
  helper — do not hand-roll tmp+rename); the old snapshot goes to `<snap>.bak`, deleted only after a clean replay
  round-trip in the same run. `.bak` CRASH RECOVERY (resolved): if `<snap>.bak` exists at rebuild start, RE-REBUILD
  from the full log (authoritative) and overwrite — do not "restore from .bak". Log (WARN) each detected orphan +
  rebuild; a rebuild counter lives as a module-level metric in fsck. Concurrency: a single-writer guard (the store
  write-lock) serializes fsck rebuild vs compaction on a ticket.

## Dependencies
- A1 (tricolour-head-ratfish) ADDS `RETIRED_SUFFIX`/`is_active_event`/the `.retired` rename (NOT in the codebase
  today) — depends_on A1 landing first (wired). A3 (entitled-unsweet-nutria) REUSES this `include_retired` rebuild.

## Scope
`reducer/_processors.py` (process_snapshot + positional skip ~648-702), `reducer/_api.py` + `reducer/_cache.py`
(`include_retired` + cache bypass), `_commands/fsck.py` (orphan-detect + rebuild + counter), `_commands/compact.py`
(horizon fold guard), `_config_schema.py` (`CompactConfig.COMPACTION_HORIZON_NS` + `_SECTIONS['compact']`),
`config.py` (env alias). `reducer/_sort.py` read-only. NOT the reconciler code.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] `COMPACTION_HORIZON_NS` on `CompactConfig` (default 1_800_000_000_000 ns), coercer in `_SECTIONS['compact']`, env alias in `config.py`; ADR records the rule. Test: an event younger than the horizon is NOT folded.
- [ ] `reduce_ticket`/`prepare_event_files`/`scan_for_latest_snapshot` gain `include_retired`; default False preserves behavior; the rebuild path bypasses the cache. Test: rebuild does not return a stale cached state.
- [ ] Rebuild uses `fsutil.atomic_write`; `.bak` written + deleted only after a clean round-trip; if `.bak` exists at start, re-rebuild (test covers the interrupted-rebuild restart); rebuild counter incremented + WARN logged.
- [ ] A merged-in pre-horizon orphan is no longer silently dropped. Two-clone sub-horizon-append regression test: RED before, GREEN after.
- [ ] Determinism proof: `reduce_ticket` twice on the same file set (incl. shuffled `os.listdir` order) -> equal compiled_state. Proof: `python -m pytest tests/scripts/reducer/ -k determinism -q`.
- [ ] Decision + design recorded in an ADR.

file_impact.reason: stop positional skip dropping merged-in orphans

compiled_state.title: Snapshot-horizon-safe reconciliation (RC2b): no silent sub-horizon drop

verify_commands.dd_text: merged-in pre-horizon orphan no longer dropped; reducer + concurrency suites green
